comment: I don’t usually write reviews, but after using GitHub Copilot for 2 years, I felt I should share this. The recent version has been frustrating — it’s buggy and often hits rate limit errors that break my workflow. It also feels like some useful features and models, like Claude, that I relied on are no longer available. Because of this, I’m considering trying other tools and hope these issues get fixed soon.

comment: Things became better since 2024, but now a different problem raises: Copilot loses to other competitors. The only modern LLM it has to offer is Haiku 4.5, while others available are obsoleted by now. Why should I give it my API keys to use up-to-date LLMs in there when I can use official integrations, like Codex or Claude Code? It doesn't seem like Copilot is doing super-magic under the hood that out-weights competitors. Their coding assistants are good too!

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Ok, here we go. I normally do not write reviews. As a pycharm lover, I of course used the plugin and was wondering why the reviews are soooo bad. And then it started... Weird stuff was happening.

  • freezes
  • no good memory
  • slow ide
  • bullshit code generation for larger projects
  • requests just failed
  • and much more

Just, and I am sorry about this, in every way bad. And then because of some comments here, I switched to VScode. And WOW, this is soooooo much better. I had no idea how much fun this is. Its night and day. Smooth, no bugs, good code.

I am sorry about this jetbrains. You made one of the best IDEs and in the times of AI coding you messed up big time. You will probably loose most of your customers if you do not MASSIVELY overhaul this.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Lately, this has been one of the most frequently used IDE plugins. It helps me with literally everything – managing agents, adding lines, naming commits, and conducting reviews. It's simply superb!

One inconvenience: agents constantly forget they can read and write files and try to output something to themselves via heredoc, which just freezes in the pyCharm console. I have to pause the conversation and explain, "Don't use heredoc, you can read and write directly."

Sometimes I want to have multiple conversations running in parallel, rather than just one in focus. For example, one is preparing a review, while the other is changing something. Currently, only one is available. I have to maintain a second copy of the project for parallelization.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
• In the latest release(s), trying a very simple prompt in "Agent" mode starts running with "Plan is working with auto language model" and runs for 10 times longer as it normally would.
  • When I click on a diff in the list of edited files (just above the chat input field), it focuses the edited file but doesn't jump to any of the edited lines.
  • When I click on the "Diff" button on that line, it doesn´t do anything more than just clicking on that line.
  • There is no side-by-side diff view like in VS Code's Copilot.
  • Can't retry a prompt after its execution, or branch from a point in a conversation.

The VS Code extension doesn't have any of these issues.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
GitHub has announced they are going to use Copilot requests to harvest code from individual users for training their models, without regard for the public/private status or content policies of any repos those users are interacting with when using Copilot, and give organizations no way to prevent that use.

For that reason we are having to prohibit Copilot use and require individuals apply the "opt-out" setting (on a per-user individual basis), in my organization.

Before they did that, Copilot was borderline useful for a few things but not the best model for code assistance. With the change it's a mid-tier model and a crowdsourced content security threat that we can't disable.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
I used to be very anti-ai until I gave this plugin a try in both Rider and Visual Studio (as well as Chat GPT / Codex). A year later, I can say it is a tremendous time saver and the models keep getting better and better.

My only nitpick is that the version of this plugin for Rider / IntelliJ sometimes stops generating completion suggestions, even if I type a comment and wait for a bit. This problem doesn't happen with Visual Studio's version.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: It's baffling how big the gap is between VSCode's GitHub Copilot and this plugin. The performance reduces with longer histories. So restoring an old session first takes up to a minute post launch. This plugin also shares the same issue that Visual Studio GitHub Copilot has; there's no guarantee that the revert function will properly revert the codebase. There's always a chance that it scrambles it. Models aren't provided with sufficient IDE tools, or context about the cli environment. Which means that the models, without further MCP tools, rely on cli guesswork. Git doesn't have any separate approval system. If you approve all git commands, there's a chance the model will make commits or resets without user approval. So it's best to use a MCP server for git. I also see tons of server-related issues, and the plugin doesn't gracefully deal with those. So there's no repeat function. Instead you have to continue the conversation, because the revert button doesn't show up.
